title: phlegmariurus recurvifolius
intro: Phlegmariurus recurvifolius, also known as the creeping forked horsetail, is a type of epiphytic or terrestrial plant that belongs to the Lycopodiaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Phlegmariurus recurvifolius include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory problems, and treat skin conditions such as eczema and wounds. The plant is typically prepared for medicinal use through herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be administered orally or topically to harness its therapeutic properties.
